stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Termites can construct tall mounds that maintain a stable interior temperature even in scorching deserts.
  • In 2019, engineer Lucia Patel was inspired by termite mounds to design self-cooling building modules.
  • Over the next two years, she designed, prototyped, tested, and refined her modules.
  • Each module includes a network of internal vents that release warm air through openings at the top.
  • Cooler air is drawn in through openings at the base, lowering the interior temperature without using electricity.
stem: The student wants to begin a narrative about the creation of the building modules. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Inspired by termites, which build tall mounds that maintain a stable interior temperature in scorching deserts, Lucia Patel set out to design building modules that could cool themselves in a similar way.
Within each of Lucia Patel’s modules, warm air rises through internal vents and exits at the top while cooler air is drawn in at the base.
Over two years, Lucia Patel designed, prototyped, tested, and refined her building modules.
Termites, which have inspired the design of building modules, construct mounds that maintain a stable temperature.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The sentence effectively begins a narrative about the creation of the modules, explaining that termites’ cooling technique inspired Patel to “set out to design” building modules that could cool themselves in a similar way.

Choice B is incorrect. The sentence describes how the modules work; it doesn’t effectively begin a narrative about their creation. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence summarizes the design process; it doesn’t effectively begin the narrative. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence describes termite behavior; it doesn’t effectively begin the narrative about the modules’ creation.
